items.title: N9 polls: Pas instructs members to support BN candidates
items.summary: TAMPIN: Pas has instructed its members to support Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in the Negri Sembilan election, in a move reflecting closer cooperation between the two coalitions. Pas secretary-general Takiyuddin Hassan called on party members in the Tampin parliamentary constituency to vote for both BN and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidates contesting in three state seats in the Aug 1 polls. The candidates are PN's Ridzuan Ahmad in Gemas, BN's Suhaimizan Bizar in Gemencheh and BN's Koh Kim Swee in Repah.

items.title: MDEC to revoke digital status of Forest City school operator NSO Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) is taking immediate steps to revoke the Malaysia Digital status of NSO Malaysia Sdn Bhd. NSO operates Network School in Forest City, Iskandar Puteri, Johor. “Accordingly, MDEC is taking immediate action to revoke NSO’s Malaysia Digital status,” it said.

items.title: Oil prices rise slightly after US announces new round of strikes on Iran
items.summary: Brent crude futures rose 0.55 per cent, or 50 cents, to US$91.51 after markets opened on Wednesday, while US West Texas Intermediate crude climbed 0.36 per cenr, or 30 cents, to US$84.64 in low volume trading. This comes after oil settled at a five-week high on Tuesday after US forces struck targets in southern and western Iran, while Iran attacked US facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan. The US military said it began its latest strikes on Iran late on Tuesday in the United States, or early Wednesday in Iran.

items.title: Hundreds flee as wildfire rips through southern France
items.summary: COTIGNAC, France: A fast-moving wildfire erupted Tuesday in southern France, forcing hundreds out of their homes as nearly 1,000 firefighters and water-bombing aircraft battled to stop the blaze. The fire swept through wooded hills in the Var region, burning 1,700 hectares in just a few hours and prompting the evacuation of nearly 400 people, the area's top official said. He said evacuations took place around the villages of Cotignac, Montfort-sur-Argens and Correns.

items.title: Moroccan man's death during arrest sparks protests, probe in Italy
items.summary: ROME: Morocco sought a "full clarification", and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Tuesday called for "the truth right to the end" after a Moroccan man died during an arrest, igniting protests. The tragedy occurred after the man was tackled to the ground by police. Morocco's foreign ministry said it had asked the Italian authorities to "fully clarify" the death and determine responsibility and take appropriate legal measures.

items.title: 'PBN' coined to reflect PN-BN pact in Negri polls, says Hamzah
items.summary: TAMPIN: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has coined the acronym "PBN" to describe its electoral understanding with Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Negri Sembilan state election. Parti Wawasan Negara president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin said the label was intended to avoid confusion among voters. Also present were PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee, Tampin Umno division chief Datuk Mohd Isam Mohd Isa, Gemencheh BN candidate Suhaimizan Bizar, and Gemas PN candidate Ridzuan Ahmad.

items.title: At least 53 dead in Guyana capsized ferry disaster
items.summary: GEORGETOWN, Guyana: At least 53 people died after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana over the weekend, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Tuesday. The MV Barima was traveling from the capital Georgetown to Port Kaituma in Guyana's inland Essequibo region when it flipped over on Saturday night. A desperate search for survivors began early Sunday, with 76 people rescued so far, according to Phillips, after the government previously said 77 had been located alive.

items.title: N9 polls: PN-BN pact sealed four days before nomination day, says Takiyuddin
items.summary: TAMPIN: Perikatan Nasional secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan revealed that the understanding between the coalition and Barisan Nasional (BN) for the Negri Sembilan state election was finalised just four days before nomination day. The Pas secretary-general said the agreement was concluded by himself, Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, and Negri Sembilan Umno leaders. "Datuk Seri Hamzah, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi, the Negri Sembilan Umno leadership and I finalised this understanding four days before nomination day.

items.title: Nike to tighten online sales in China amid 'cluttered' marketplace
items.summary: NEW YORK: Nike is trying to lure back shoppers in China by controlling how its products are sold online, directing consumers to official Nike channels as the American sportswear giant continues to lose ground to domestic rivals. By restricting wholesale distributors' online sales, the company aims to rebuild trust with Chinese shoppers and sell its products at full price, said Cathy Sparks, vice president and general manager of Greater China. CHINA SALES DECLINE PERSISTS China, Nike's third-largest market, remains a key source of concern for the world's biggest sportswear brand.

items.title: US judge blocks Trump administration from stripping immigrants of work permits
items.summary: BOSTON: A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump's administration from stripping tens of thousands of asylum seekers and immigrants who have Temporary Protected Status of their ability to work in the United States. US District Judge Nathaniel Gorton in Boston sided with a coalition of immigrant rights groups and labour unions that sued to block US Citizenship and Immigration Services from moving forward with a series of policies designed to implement new immigration-related restrictions enacted by Congress last year. Democratic former President Joe Biden's administration extended TPS for people from El Salvador, Sudan and Ukraine in January 2025.

items.title: MAA lifts 2026 car sales forecast as EV, SUV demand accelerates
items.summary: MAA had raised the full-year total industry volume (TIV) to 800,000 units from 790,000 units, its president Mohd Shamsor Mohd Zain told a briefing on Tuesday. This has given Malaysia's automotive industry more confidence to plan production, investments and sales strategies, MAA added. MAA has also raised its electrified vehicle sales forecast, expecting all EV sales to reach 120,000 units, accounting for 15 per cent of total industry sales, compared with its earlier estimate of 100,000 units.

items.title: Canada cancels joint bridge celebration with US, citing Trump trade threats
items.summary: OTTAWA: Canada has canceled a planned joint celebration with the US to mark the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge between Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit, citing trade threats from President Donald Trump. The bridge is scheduled to open to traffic on July 27. Canada is still "committed" to opening the bridge on July 27 but will mark the milestone by celebrating "among Canadians" on July 24, Ghassabeh said.

items.title: Trump: US airlines can now fly to Lebanon for first time since 1985
items.summary: NEW YORK: President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, after meeting with Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun, that he was directing the US government to allow all domestic airline carriers to fly directly to that country. It was unclear if US airlines would be willing to resume flights. The major US airlines, American Airlines, United Airlines and Delta Air Lines, have halted flights to nearby Tel Aviv, citing security concerns.

items.title: Trump slaps 100pct tariff on imported generic drugs from 2028
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday the imposition of steep tariffs on imported generic drugs from August 2028, a move he said was aimed at onshoring production of such pharmaceutical products. "Effective August 1st, 2026, all Generic Drugs being brought into the United States will continue to have a TARIFF of ZERO PERCENT for a two year period of time, after which the TARIFF will be raised to 100% for a one year period of time, and 200% thereafter," he said in a post on his Truth Social platform.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: US war in Iran has cost US$37.5 billion so far: Pentagon
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States' war in Iran has cost US$37.5 billion so far, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday, an increase of nearly US$8 billion since the last public estimate. It ⁠was unclear how the Pentagon arrived at the US$37.5 billion figure. A source told Reuters in March that ⁠President Donald Trump's administration estimated the first six days of the war had cost at least US$11.3 billion.

items.title: Petaling Jaya’s leasehold dilemma: High premiums, shrinking buyers and ageing leases
items.summary: Currently, leasehold property owners in Petaling Jaya have two options: Option 1: A property owner can pay a nominal fee of RM5,000 and apply to extend the lease up to 99 years. Option 2: Should the property owner intend to sell the property, he or she has to renew the lease for 99 years and pay the full premium based on this formula: [¼ x 1/100 x Value of land x 99 years x Area of Land] The property owner is entitled to a 30 per cent discount, but the premium must be settled within six months after Form 5A for lease renewal is issued. Rajesh said many property leases in Sections 1 to 18 would expire in about 42 years, with some properties having only 20 or 25 years remaining.

items.title: Trump set to hit dozens of nations with new tariffs
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States is set to impose new tariffs that could hit dozens of countries soon, trade envoy Jamieson Greer signaled Tuesday, with President Donald Trump's temporary global levies due to expire this week. Analysts expect that new tariffs over forced labour concerns – set between 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent – would replace these temporary duties. - FORCED LABOUR CONCERNS - Greer said Tuesday that new action on forced labour will cover the majority of US trade.

items.title: MRSM students turn fish bones into calcium supplement
items.summary: SIX students and a biology teacher from Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) Pasir Salak have developed a calcium supplement using the bones of the suckermouth catfish or known locally as 'ikan bandaraya'. MRSM Pasir Salak biology teacher Mahfuza Mansor said the idea stemmed from research conducted with students two years ago using chicken bones. "Following a five-month research, we successfully processed chicken bones into calcium supplement powder," she said, adding that the project went on to win a gold medal at the national-level Idea'24 competition in September 2024.

items.title: 'MCMC has blocked 3.04b suspicious texts since 2022'
items.summary: THE Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), in collaboration with telecommunications service providers, blocked a total of 3.04 billion suspicious text messages and 2.5 billion scam calls between Jan 1, 2022 and June 30 this year. "MCMC also takes regulatory and enforcement action under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 and Online Safety Act 2025," he said in a written parliamentary reply to Datuk Seri S. Vell Paari. "MCMC monitors harmful online content under Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act, while the Online Safety Act empowers the commission to act against content involving financial fraud," he added.

items.title: Why Hormuz, Malacca and key maritime straits matter in global conflicts
items.summary: PARIS: Hormuz and a handful of maritime straits around the world have served as the linchpin of global trade since the 16th century and have fuelled geopolitical rivalries throughout history. In Hormuz, Iran, which "has not ratified the Montego Bay Convention, had until now respected it under customary law," Domergue said. "To guarantee freedom of navigation, one had to start by guaranteeing freedom of passage through the straits," Domergue said.

items.title: Stratus targets bigger order book
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Newly listed Stratus Global Holdings Bhd expects to double its order book within the next 12 months. Executive director and chief executive officer Ryo Narisawa said the company had emerged from a relatively slow period and was seeing stronger interest from major customers. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: UN: Scam gangs cost Asia Pacific US$88b in 2025
items.summary: SCAM gangs defrauded Asia Pacific residents of at least US$88 billion last year, said a United Nations office on Tuesday, with transnational crime groups rapidly evolving tactics to stay ahead of law enforcement. The report estimated scam losses across East Asia, Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand at between US$88.3 billion and US$114.1 billion for 2025, a figure larger than the gross domestic product of several of the nations in the region. Scam operators were increasingly seeking recruits from outside Asia as they expanded into new markets, said UNODC.

items.title: Trump warns US 'not finished' with Iran as Gulf conflict widens
items.summary: Two weeks after fighting resumed between the decades-old foes, Trump said Iran would need more than 20 years to recover from the damage inflicted in the Middle East war. "If we left right now, it would take Iran 20, 25 years to rebuild," he said. Bahrain said it intercepted Iranian attacks as sirens sounded repeatedly, while Kuwait and Jordan said they had intercepted drone and missile strikes.

items.title: Hundreds battle new mega-wildfire in Spain
items.summary: TAMAJON: More than 600 firefighters and troops and 29 aircraft on Tuesday battled for a sixth day a huge wildfire north of Madrid that authorities said is one of the most difficult they have faced. Some 1,200 people have been evacuated from their homes and the flames have so far scorched 30,000 hectares of land, officials said. Firefighters said that 34 isolated hamlets had been evacuated across Castilla-La Mancha.

items.title: Aminuddin: Zahid's remarks a challenge to Anwar's authority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has described Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's remarks challenging Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to sack Barisan Nasional ministers as inappropriate. Free Malaysia Today reported that Aminuddin said Zahid's statement appeared to challenge the prime minister's authority. Aminuddin said Anwar's statement was merely a reminder to ensure ties within the unity government remained intact.

items.title: Man arrested for killing brother during argument
items.summary: KUALA KUBU BARU: Police on Monday arrested a man for killing his sibling during a heated argument here. Hulu Selangor police chief Superintendent M. Manimaran said the suspect, 26, was ordered by the magistrate's court to be placed under seven-day remand from today. He said police were still investigating the motive behind the killing and the cause of the argument.

items.title: Global growth could near zero over next century - PMO adviser
items.summary: IPOH: Global economic growth could slow to near zero over the next century as rising trade barriers, intensifying geopolitical competition and supply chain disruptions reshape the global economy, Economic Adviser to the Prime Minister's Office Nurhisham Hussein said. He said the changing environment requires countries, including Malaysia, to integrate national security considerations into broader economic policymaking to strengthen resilience against external shocks. This is because Islamic finance, every financial transaction has an underlying economic transaction, it will drive economic growth," she said.

items.title: Add 'harms test' to FOI Bill to protect public interest, says Malaysian Bar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar has urged the Parliamentary Special Select Committee (PSSC) to overhaul the proposed Freedom of Information (FOI) Bill. Its president Anand Raj said the bill focuses excessively on procedures for obtaining information held by public authorities, even though such processes could be implemented through internal guidelines and standard operating procedures without legislation. "The bill must include a 'harms test', requiring public officers to assess whether withholding information would cause greater harm than the public interest served by disclosure before rejecting a request.

items.title: NST Leader: School bullying - Time for accountability, not excuses
items.summary: ASUCCESSION of bullying cases, culminating in a Form One student suffering a fractured ankle and the revelation that a disabled teenager dropped out of school after enduring relentless taunts, underscores the persistence of a problem that Malaysia has struggled to eradicate despite repeated promises and campaigns. Those who are tasked with putting an end to bullying in schools must be reminded that education never happens in an unsafe learning environment. Loss of one life to bullying is one too many.

items.title: Monthly assembly at night reflects round-the-clock policing, says KL's top cop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur police's decision to hold its monthly assembly at night reflects the reality of policing a capital city that never sleeps. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the initiative, held for the second time, underscored the force's 24-hour operational readiness, with most crime cases, intelligence-gathering, investigations and enforcement operations in the federal capital taking place after dark. "Kuala Lumpur operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and the night is among the busiest periods in a metropolitan city like this.

items.title: Turkiye's opposition leader to form new party to challenge Erdogan
items.summary: ANKARA: Turkiye's opposition leader, Ozgur Ozel, said on Tuesday he will resign from the main opposition party and form a new one, amid a nearly two-year long legal crackdown on President Tayyip Erdogan's political opponents. The move, which was expected, poses the latest challenge for the Republican People's Party's (CHP), which modern Turkiye's founder Mustafa Kemal Ataturk had established more than a century ago. Most CHP voters and parliamentarians are expected to follow Ozel to the unnamed new party, after a court in May stripped him of his title as chairman by annulling the CHP's 2023 congress.

items.title: Bystander wounded near White House to sue Secret Service, says lawyer
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Lawyers for a bystander wounded in a shootout between the US Secret Service and a man outside the White House in May said they would file a personal injury lawsuit against the federal agency on Tuesday. Del Real, who was shot in the abdomen, said the gunman never faced him but Secret Service agents fired many rounds in his direction across a busy intersection, the law firm said. Del Real underwent life-saving surgery at a local hospital, it said.

items.title: PM Anwar calls on immigration personnel to uphold integrity, reject corruption
items.summary: In a Facebook post in conjunction with Immigration Day today, Anwar said that, in addition to upholding integrity, JIM personnel must always embrace the value of compassion in delivering efficient, courteous and professional services. “Happy Immigration Day to all personnel of the Immigration Department. The 2026 Immigration Day celebration is themed “Charting a New Face for Immigration”.

items.title: Sabah government says in midst of proposing upgrade of BUDI diesel subsidy scheme
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — Citing the difficulties it has caused for people in the state, the state government intends to give its feedback and present new proposals to the federal government on the targeted diesel subsidy scheme that limits eligible recipients to a 200-litre monthly quota. State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the many difficulties faced by Sabahans was the basis for establishing a special task force to review the scheme before formally submitting improvement proposals to the federal government. He said this in response to Likas assemblyman Tham Yun Fook who asked whether the Sabah government officially supported the current 200-litre diesel subsidy policy.

items.title: Sabah NGO president arrested by MACC over alleged RM2m fund misappropriation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested the president of a Sabah-based non-governmental organisation (NGO) on suspicion of abusing his position by misappropriating about RM2 million allocated for the construction of a community hall. When contacted, Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah confirmed the arrest. Mohd Fuad added that the suspect is expected to be charged at the Kota Kinabalu Special Corruption Court on Thursday.

items.title: Sabah denies RM210m GLC allocation as bailout, instead strategic investments without new cashflow
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — Sabah has defended its RM210 million allocation for state-linked companies, rejecting claims that the government was giving “bailouts” but instead said they were strategic investments that had undergone financial evaluation and risk assessment. State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the allocation under the Supplementary Supply Bill 2026 involved RM200 million for Sabah Development Bank Berhad (SDBank) and RM10 million for POIC Sabah Sdn Bhd. Masidi who is also deputy chief minister II said the RM200 million allocation for SDBank did not involve any new cash injection from the state government, but was instead a phased accounting adjustment to convert existing fixed deposits held by the government into redeemable preference shares (RPS).

items.title: Penang aims for 100pc cashless transactions
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 21 — The Penang government is committed to achieving 100 per cent cashless transactions in the near future through various initiatives currently being implemented. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said one of the key initiatives involves the participation of all 24 state government departments and agencies in a campaign promoting digital payments to expand the adoption of cashless transactions among the public. He said the achievement marked Penang’s second consecutive win, following its 95 per cent cashless transaction rate under the previous Cashless Boleh 4.0 campaign.

items.title: Police bust international scam syndicate targeting US citizens in Kuantan raid
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 21 —Police arrested 16 individuals, including eight women, believed to be involved in an online scam syndicate during a raid on a premises near Jalan Tengku Muhammad here yesterday. Acting Pahang police chief DCP Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the suspects, comprising 12 Philippine nationals, two locals, and two Taiwanese nationals aged between 24 and 51, were nabbed at about 11 pm. “Preliminary investigations found that the two Taiwanese men were the masterminds, while the two local men acted as runners and drivers.

items.title: Sarawak boat tragedy: Three-year-old found dead, three missing after vessel capsizes in Baram
items.summary: MIRI, July 21 — A three-year-old child was found dead, while three others remain missing after a boat carrying the victims reportedly capsized in Sungai Seperot near Long San, Baram. The victim’s body was taken to the Long San Health Clinic,” the department said in a statement. According to the police report, the four victims, all from Kampung Long Selatong, had left by boat for the Long San Health Clinic yesterday (July 20) but failed to return home and could not be contacted.

items.title: Post-mortem report still pending in case of army captain charged over fatal MEX Highway crash involving Bangladeshi passenger
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The prosecution has completed handing over documents for the case involving a military captain accused of driving under the influence of alcohol and causing a fatal crash that killed a Bangladeshi e-hailing passenger. However, defence lawyer Muhammad Nazreen Jaafar Abdullah, who represents the accused, Captain Jad Faid Arhan, 31, said documents related to the alleged murder charge, including the victim’s chemical and medical reports, had yet to be received. On May 1, Jad Faid was charged with two counts of driving a white Ford Fiesta under the influence of alcohol, causing him to lose control of the vehicle.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several states including Kedah, Selangor, Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit several areas across the country, including parts of Perlis, Kedah, Perak, Selangor, Johor and Sarawak, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In an advisory issued at 3.45pm today, MetMalaysia said the weather conditions were expected to persist until 6pm in affected areas. In Selangor, affected areas include Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat, while Kuala Lumpur has also been placed under the thunderstorm warning.

items.title: Zahid tells BN machinery to work harder, admits party is ‘underdog’ in N. Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has admitted he is not satisfied with the party’s preparations ahead of the 16th Negri Sembilan state election, urging its machinery and candidates to step up their efforts. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said BN’s candidates and election machinery must intensify their efforts throughout the campaign period to improve the party’s chances. At today’s event, Ahmad Zahid also presented Excellent Service Awards to recipients from the Kemas headquarters, Federal Territory, Negri Sembilan, Melaka, Kemas Kuala Lumpur Skills Institute (ILKKL) and Kemas Training Centre (PLK) Melaka.

items.title: Forest City Network School licence revoked, Johor warns no one is above the law
items.summary: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the decision was made following reviews of inspection reports, enforcement findings and representations submitted by the company, in accordance with existing laws and regulations. Onn Hafiz said the duration a premises had been operating did not mean all its activities were automatically compliant with licence requirements, adding that action must be taken once violations were identified. “When non-compliance is identified, action must be taken without compromise.

items.title: Rise in Social Visit Pass abuse triggers immigration crackdown
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, July 21 — The Immigration Department (JIM) has stepped up enforcement operations following a rise in the misuse of Social Visit Passes (SVPs) by foreign nationals, its director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said. He said Immigration records showed 1,279 arrests for SVP-related offences in 2023, rising to 2,644 in 2024 before easing slightly to 2,630 in 2025. We will continue pursuing those who abuse their immigration passes, particularly individuals who commit offences involving the misuse of these passes,” he told reporters after the 2026 Immigration Day celebration, themed ‘Charting a New Face of Immigration’, graced by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

items.title: ‘I have nothing to do with it’: Azam Baki denies role in RM178m contract awarded to brother-linked firm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has denied having any involvement in the award of a RM178.29 million contract to a company linked to his brother, Datuk Nasir Baki. According to Sinar Harian , Azam said he was unaware of the matter and stressed that the contract award was a business matter involving the company and the relevant ministry. “It is up to the ministry to award the project to his company,” he said.

items.title: Motorists who ignore licence bans could face three-year prison term, says deputy transport minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Motorists who continue driving despite having their licences suspended or revoked by the court could face up to three years in prison under the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the amendment to Section 32 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) was aimed at increasing the existing penalties to encourage compliance with the law and reduce the risk of road accidents. “For electric motor vehicles, the amendment clarifies that such vehicles must have a clear, visible and untampered electric motor vehicle serial number and chassis number,” he said.

items.title: Tawau court jails tuition teacher 105 years, 70 strokes for repeatedly raping daughter; total sentence hits 280 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 53‑year‑old tuition teacher from Lahad Datu was sentenced by the Tawau Sessions Court to 105 years in prison and 70 strokes of the cane after pleading guilty to seven counts of repeatedly raping his eldest daughter between December 2025 and March 2026. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the punishment, ordering 15 years’ jail and 10 strokes for each charge, to run consecutively, Berita Harian reported. In total, he now faces 280 years in prison and 180 strokes of the cane.

items.title: Tamim Dahri pleads not guilty to charge of damaging sacred Hindu object
items.summary: LANGKAWI, July 21 — An activist pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of damaging a sacred Hindu object at an old temple site in March. Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak, 34, entered his plea before Magistrate Hemy Annerina Haja Mydin. However, lawyer Datuk Hanif Hassan, representing Tamim Dahri, applied for bail, stating that the offence under Section 295 of the Penal Code is bailable.

items.title: Teen suffers fractured skull, bleeding in head and ear after alleged assault in Cheras; four arrested
items.summary: Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud said police detected the circulation of a video recording of the incident on WhatsApp, Sinar Harian reported today. “A team from the Cheras district police headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Division subsequently arrested four local male teenagers, aged 14, to assist investigations,” he said in a statement today. Mohd Rosdi said police also seized a mobile phone belonging to one of the suspects, believed to have been used to record the incident.

items.title: Man detained in Tumpat after homemade air rifle found in car
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 27-year-old man was detained after a homemade air rifle and 61 metal pellets were found in a car he was travelling in at Kampung Parit Bendang Morak, Tumpat, Kelantan, this morning. The man allegedly claimed the air rifle was used to hunt foxes, which he later sold in the Tumpat area. He said the team approached the vehicle, but the suspect sped off after noticing their presence.

items.title: Hadi defends PAS-Umno cooperation, cites need for Malay-Muslim unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the party was “forced” to cooperate with Umno as part of efforts to unite Malays and Muslims, warning that continued division could weaken their political position. In his latest “Minda Presiden PAS” article, Abdul Hadi rejected claims that cooperation between Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) was merely political manoeuvring, saying such alliances should instead be viewed as efforts to strengthen unity. He said BA, formed in 1999, involved PAS, Parti Keadilan Nasional, Parti Rakyat Malaysia and DAP, but the coalition later weakened after disagreements emerged, including over PAS’ push for an Islamic state.

items.title: Housing Ministry prioritising urgent repairs for ageing PPR projects amid budget cuts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is prioritising the maintenance of People’s Housing Programme (PPR) projects that are more than 10 years old, while focusing on critical defects that affect the safety and comfort of residents. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said priority is given to issues involving lifts, roofs, water tanks and reticulation systems, sanitary piping, electrical wiring, as well as general repairs involving common property. Aiman Athirah said that under the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), KPKT had spent RM159.1 million through Rolling Plans One to Five for the maintenance of high-rise strata PPR projects nationwide.

items.title: Man remanded two days from today over alleged insulting remarks against Negri Sembilan royal institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 52-year-old man has been remanded for two days to assist investigations into a social media post allegedly containing insulting remarks and comments deemed sensitive towards the Negri Sembilan royal institution. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the man was remanded from today to facilitate investigations under Section 4 of the Sedition Act 1948, Section 505 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, according to a report published by Kosmo! It was previously reported that the man was arrested at about 1pm yesterday over a post uploaded on the Threads application, which was believed to contain remarks that insulted and touched on sensitivities involving the Negri Sembilan royal institution.

items.title: High Court to decide on Papagomo sedition appeal on July 31
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The High Court today fixed July 31 to deliver its decision on the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, on a sedition charge dating back to 2023. Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor set the date after hearing submissions from Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP) Mohamad Mustaffa P. Kunyalam and defence counsel Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, representing Wan Muhammad Azri. Earlier, Mohamad Mustaffa argued that the charge against Wan Muhammad Azri was not defective, despite the first prosecution witness, Hisham Sharudin, who was also the complainant, having withdrawn his police report.

items.title: Maxis probes unauthorised access to customer’s account after social media post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Telecommunications company Maxis Bhd is investigating an incident involving a customer’s account that was circulated on social media, saying the matter was an isolated case involving unauthorised access. In a statement posted on its social media platforms today, Maxis said all access to customer accounts is fully logged and monitored. “Based on our findings, this is an isolated incident involving an unauthorised action.

items.title: Malaysia sees rise in foreigners misusing social visit passes to work, says Immigration Dept
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The Immigration Department has detected an increase in cases involving foreigners misusing social visit passes to work or conduct economic activities in Malaysia in recent years. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 1,279 arrests were made in 2023, rising to 2,644 cases in 2024 and 2,630 cases last year, Kosmo! He said 451 arrests had been recorded as of July 15 this year.

items.title: Myanmar factory worker charged with murdering compatriot in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A Myanmar factory worker was charged in the Magistrates’ Court in Klang today with the murder of his fellow countryman earlier this month. The accused, 32-year-old Maung Maung, nodded in understanding after the charge was read to him in Myanmar through an interpreter before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat, according to a report published by Kosmo! According to the charge, Maung is accused of murdering Myo Than Naing at a premises along Jalan Tengah, Kampung Tengah Telok Gong, Port Klang, between 9.30pm and 10pm on July 5.

items.title: DBKL ramps up tree mitigation works to improve public safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is continuing tree mitigation works across several locations in the capital as part of ongoing efforts to improve public safety and reduce the risk posed by urban trees. In a Facebook post today, DBKL said this week’s works will focus on the parliamentary constituencies of Bukit Bintang, Lembah Pantai and Batu. The works will be carried out at Selera Medan Bunus (Lestari Niaga), Jalan Pantai Murni in Pantai Dalam, Jalan Pantai Permai in Bangsar, Jalan Haji Salleh in Sentul, Jalan Sinar Sentul in Sentul, and the JPK Towing Depot along Jalan Sentul.

items.title: Heavy rain, local factors caused Petaling Jaya flash floods, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The flash floods that hit several areas in Petaling Jaya on Saturday were likely caused by exceptionally heavy rainfall over a short period, which exceeded the capacity of existing drainage systems, according to Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim. Izham said preliminary assessments also pointed to localised factors as contributing to the flooding despite various flood mitigation measures, including the construction of the FAS Tunnel. Izham said the state government would continue strengthening its flood early warning system to ensure residents receive timely information and have adequate time to prepare.

items.title: DPM Zahid denies claims of BN–Perikatan–GPS–GRS meeting, reaffirms support for unity government
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 21 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has dismissed viral claims that leaders from Barisan Nasional (BN), Perikatan Nasional (PN), Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) and Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) were scheduled to meet today. According to Berita Harian , the BN chairman said he had no knowledge of any such gathering and stressed that BN’s position remains firmly aligned with the Unity Government until the 16th General Election (GE16). Zahid, who is also Umno president, said BN’s stance is consistent with resolutions adopted at the Umno General Assembly.
